data.title: Ancora seeking US Steel board records, ratcheting up proxy fight - Reuters
data.description:
Activist investor Ancora Holdings, which launched a boardroom challenge at US Steel (NYSE:X) last month, is demanding access to company records from board minutes to financial documents, Reuters reported Tuesday, as it ramps up a campaign to replace some of the steel company's board members and chief executive.

Ancora reportedly told US Steel (NYSE:X) it wants to investigate "potential wrongdoing in connection with ... the company's futile [lawsuit]... and the unusual trading plan of the company's CEO," David Burritt, and is looking for information to determine whether the board violated its fiduciary duties by filing the lawsuit and to determine whether Burritt "sought to trade on material non-public information."

The investor currently owns ~500K shares, or less than 1%, in US Steel (X) but has said it plans to increase its position significantly, has nominated nine director candidates to the steel company's 12-person board, including an executive who could replace Burritt, and wants the company to drop the lawsuit where it is asking a federal appeals court to overturn President Biden's decision to scuttle the merger deal with Nippon Steel.

By pursuing the lawsuit, US Steel (X) is hurting shareholders, Ancora said, noting it wants management and the board to concentrate on fixing the business.

data.title: Ultragenyx granted FDA priority review for gene therapy in rare brain disorder
data.description:
• Ultragenyx Pharma (NASDAQ:RARE) announced Tuesday that the U.S. FDA granted priority review for its marketing application for its gene therapy candidate UX111 (ABO-102) targeted at Sanfilippo syndrome type A, a rare disorder mainly affecting the brain.
  • Its Biologics License Application (BLA) seeking accelerated approval for UX111 is backed by data from the company’s ongoing pivotal Transpher A trial and long-term follow-up studies, Ultragenyx (NASDAQ:RARE) said.
  • Also known as MPS IIIA, Sanfilippo syndrome type A is a lysosomal storage disease with no FDA-approved therapies.
  • “Acceptance of the UX111 BLA brings us closer to being able to provide a first-ever treatment for Sanfilippo syndrome type A,” CEO Emil Kakkis said.
  • In connection with the review, the regulator has said it doesn’t currently plan to hold an advisory committee meeting and issued August 18, 2025, as the target action for the application.

data.title: Broadcom, Taiwan Semi going after Intel has pros and cons: analysts
data.description:
As reports swirl that Taiwan Semiconductor (NYSE:TSM) and Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) could each look to acquire parts of Intel (NASDAQ:INTC), there are pros and cons if the deals were to come to fruition, Wall Street analysts said.

Intel shares rose 10% in morning trading on Tuesday, extending recent gains. Taiwan Semiconductor slipped around 1% while Broadcom fell 2.4%.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya said such a scenario where Broadcom acquires Intel's chip design and marketing business and Taiwan Semi takes over the manufacturing business would be “time-consuming” and “complicated.” There would be “extensive” approval requirements from global regulators, given Intel's dominant market share in the PC and server CPU spaces, Arya explained.

In addition, there is a “mismatch” between Taiwan Semi and Intel's manufacturing processes, while Taiwan Semiconductor's ongoing expansion plans in Arizona could be impacted to serve customers looking for artificial intelligence processors, Arya added.

Other issues include Broadcom's high-level of debt ($58B in net debt) on its balance sheet and constraints provided by the CHIPs Act on Intel, as it is required to own 50% or more of its manufacturing capabilities as per terms of the agreement.

Pros and cons

Despite the issues related to any potential bid, there are pros and cons for any involvement from Broadcom and Taiwan Semiconductor.

If Broadcom were to get involved, the Hock Tan-led company could use its experience and expertise in increasing the efficiency of assets in “established markets” that have stick customers, Arya said.

“INTC has a very strong brand name and incumbency advantage among enterprise PC and server customers, and could add CPU capability to AVGO who has thus far focused mostly in networking, storage and accelerator silicon,” Arya wrote. “INTC’s CPU could be a possible solution for lower-cost edge AI inference workloads, complementing AVGO’s strength in cloud data centers.”

On the flip side, there is Broadcom's debt levels, the risk of “over-investing” in the x86 ecosystem, extended regulatory approval requirements and the “typical” M&A overhang that could pressure Broadcom's stock.

For Taiwan Semiconductor, there could be issues related to low levels of synergy, cultural differences, varying tool sets, geopolitics, no prior history and potential antitrust concerns, Arya said.

“Separately, if INTC is right about the impending success of its competing 18A manufacturing program, it could be reluctant to give up on it midstream to a key competitor,” Arya added.

Potential outcome?

The situation is still fluid and there are a wide array of potential structures, J.P. Morgan credit analyst Christian Crosby said. However, he believes the “ideal” outcome is for Intel to separate its foundry unit into a new company, which would include Taiwan Semiconductor taking a minority voting stake and operating responsibilities of the combined unit.

“Capital contributions from it and U.S. fabless chip names could ensure the viability of the structure, making a split much more palatable than one without partners, and Intel RemainCo would be in our view better positioned to consider deals with peers as well,” Crosby wrote in a note to clients.

Cantor Fitzgerald analyst C.J. Muse said the most likely scenario is similar to what Taiwan Semiconductor already has in Europe, via its joint venture with Bosch and NXP Semiconductor (NXPI) in the European Semiconductor Manufacturing Company.

"[European Semiconductor Manufacturing Company] is 70% owned by TSMC, and we would expect TSMC to make sure they control the equivalent [joint venture] in the US with at least 51% ownership and 100% control of the asset," Muse wrote in a note to clients. "To help fund the [joint venture], we would expect the U.S. government to participate [perhaps with the U.S. sovereign wealth fund] as well as potential equity commitments from key fabless players in the US including Apple, [Nvidia, AMD, and Qualcomm]."

(This story has been updated with additional analyst commentary.)

data.title: Axsome Therapeutics targets multiple NDA submissions and pipeline advancements through 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Axsome Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AXSM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Herriot Tabuteau highlighted that Axsome has expanded its CNS portfolio to include three commercial products and three late-stage pipeline candidates. The company aims to potentially commercialize seven products by 2027, driven by strong execution across clinical and commercial operations.
  • Total net product revenue reached $386 million for 2024, with CEO Tabuteau stating, "Based on the recent performance, Auvelity is on track to reach blockbuster potential." Symbravo received FDA approval on January 30, 2025, for acute migraine treatment, and preparations for its launch are underway.
  • CFO Nick Pizzie reported total revenues of $118.8 million for Q4, reflecting a 66% year-over-year growth. He noted that "current cash balance is sufficient to fund anticipated operations into cash flow positivity."
  • Chief Commercial Officer Ari Maizel shared that Auvelity achieved 158,000 prescriptions in Q4, marking a 10% sequential growth. Market access for Auvelity improved, covering 78% of all lives and 63% in commercial channels.

Outlook

  • Axsome plans three NDA submissions in 2025: AXS-05 for Alzheimer’s disease agitation, AXS-12 for narcolepsy with cataplexy, and AXS-14 for fibromyalgia. The NDA for AXS-14 is expected this quarter, while filings for AXS-05 and AXS-12 are projected for the second half of 2025.
  • Multiple Phase 3 trial readouts are anticipated in Q1 2025, including EMERGE for Symbravo, FOCUS for Solriamfetol in ADHD, and PARADIGM for Solriamfetol in MDD.
  • Management expects continued growth in 2025, supported by expanded salesforce efforts for Auvelity and the launch of Symbravo.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net product sales totaled $117.3 million, with $92.6 million from Auvelity and $26.2 million from Sunosi. Auvelity’s year-over-year growth was 89%, while Sunosi grew by 16%.
  • Research and development expenses were $55 million in Q4, driven by ongoing Phase 3 trials for Solriamfetol and the advancement of AXS-05 and AXS-12.
  • Selling, general, and administrative expenses increased to $113.3 million in Q4, reflecting commercialization activities for Auvelity and Sunosi.
  • Net loss for Q4 was $74.9 million, including non-cash charges of $35.9 million.

Q&A

  • Ram Selvaraju, H.C. Wainwright: Questioned the timing and strategy for direct-to-consumer (DTC) promotions for Auvelity and Symbravo. Ari Maizel responded that media spend would align with high patient flow periods in healthcare offices and further details on Symbravo’s commercial plans would follow.
  • Charles Duncan, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about synergies between the salesforce expansion for Auvelity and the upcoming Symbravo launch. Maizel explained that the 10%-15% increase in salesforce size would provide overlap in migraine treaters, particularly in primary care settings.
  • David Amsellem, Piper Sandler: Inquired about M&A targets. CFO Nick Pizzie confirmed that Axsome is exploring opportunities that complement its pipeline and leverage its commercial infrastructure.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about Axsome’s portfolio development and commercial efforts, with several questions showing interest in the upcoming NDA filings and product launches. The tone was slightly positive, with specific concerns about market access and scalability strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in both prepared remarks and responses, emphasizing the company’s operational efficiency and financial discipline. Statements like "We are very optimistic about the impact of AXS-12" reflected their confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed consistent assurance, while analysts appeared slightly more focused on commercialization challenges for new products.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 revealed expanded revenues, driven by a 10% sequential growth in Auvelity prescriptions compared to a 17% increase in Q3.
  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize the launch of Symbravo and upcoming NDA filings, while Q3 focused more on pipeline trial completions.
  • Analysts in Q4 showed heightened interest in the salesforce expansion and market access improvements for Auvelity, a shift from pipeline-specific queries in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential seasonality effects impacting Q1 2025 gross-to-net discounts for Auvelity and Sunosi, expected to rise to mid-50%.
  • Analyst concerns included competition in the migraine market for Symbravo and payer dynamics for Auvelity. Maizel noted early discussions with payers for Symbravo as "very compelling."
  • Regulatory risks were highlighted, particularly around the timing of NDA submissions for AXS-05 and AXS-12.

Final Takeaway

Axsome Therapeutics showcased strong revenue growth and a robust pipeline during its Q4 2024 earnings call. Key upcoming catalysts include three NDA submissions and multiple late-stage readouts in 2025. Auvelity's growth trajectory, paired with an enhanced salesforce and improved market access, positions the company for continued success. Meanwhile, the launch of Symbravo and advancements in pipeline assets like AXS-05 and AXS-12 are expected to drive further shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BofA's latest survey shows trade war is now biggest concern for fund managers
data.description:
Bank of America's global research team highlighted a significant shift in market sentiment in its latest fund manager survey, noting that the primary tail risk now worrying investors is not an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble, but rather the escalating trade wars initiated by President Donald Trump's tariff moves.

As per the BofA survey, 42% of respondents now see global trade war as the most bearish development for risk assets in 2025, up from January's 30%. Fund managers see "the disorderly" rise in bond yields and federal rate hikes as the second and third-most bearish developments.

BofA's survey also asked fund managers: “Which asset class would fare best under a full-blown trade war scenario?” The overwhelming answer was gold (XAUUSD:CUR) at 58%. The U.S. dollar (DXY) came in second at 15% and bitcoin (BTC-USD) only received 3% of the vote. See the chart below showing the complete breakdown:

Here are some popular exchange-traded funds that track gold, the dollar and bitcoin:

Gold ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GLDM), and (AAAU).

Dollar ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN).

Bitcoin ETFs: (IBIT), (ARKB), (GBTC), (BRRR), (BTCO), (HODL), (BTCW), (FBTC), (BITB), and (EZBC).

data.title: Gilead nabs priority review for twice yearly version of HIV med Sunlenca
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA has granted priority review to Gilead Sciences' (NASDAQ:GILD) application for a twice-yearly injectable version of its HIV drug Sunlenca (lenacapavir).
  • The FDA action date is June 19, 2025. Sunlenca was previously granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation for PrEP.
  • The application is supported by data from two phase 3 trials.

data.title: Teva and Alvotech gets FDA review acceptance for its proposed biosimilar to Eylea
data.description:
Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) and Teva Pharmaceuticals (NYSE:TEVA) said on Tuesday that the U.S. FDA has accepted for review a Biologics License Application for a biosimilar targeting Regeneron’s (REGN) blockbuster eye disease therapy, Eylea.

A biologic named AVT06, Teva (NYSE:TEVA), and Alvotech’s (NASDAQ:ALVO) biosimilar, is backed by positive data from a clinical trial for patients with neovascular (wet) AMD.

The trial, designed to compare the efficacy, safety, and immunogenicity of AVT06 with Eylea, reached the main goal, according to its topline data from January 2024. The duo said the FDA process to grant regulatory approval is expected to conclude in Q4 2025.

In addition to AVT06, Teva (TEVA) and Alvotech (ALVO) have collaborated to develop AVT29, a biosimilar targeting a high-dose version of Eylea.

Regeneron (REGN) markets Eylea in partnership with German conglomerate Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF) (OTCPK:BAYRY).

Source: Press Release

data.title: GeneDx targets $350M-$360M revenue for 2025 with 30% growth in exome and genome testing
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: GeneDx Holdings Corp. (NASDAQ:WGS) Q4 2024

Management View

  • President and CEO Katherine Stueland stated that GeneDx achieved significant milestones in 2024, including surpassing $95 million in Q4 revenue and expanding gross margins to 70%. She highlighted the company's leadership in exome and genome testing and its focus on profitable growth, supported by operational discipline and market development.

  • Stueland announced major initiatives for 2025, including investments in growth, innovation, and talent. The company plans to expand its product offerings, particularly in pediatric testing, a $25 billion market, and outlined its readiness to enter new markets such as genomic newborn screening and international expansions.

  • GeneDx launched its ultraRapid whole genome sequencing product, which delivers results within 48 hours, targeting the NICU segment. Stueland emphasized the company’s ability to address inefficiencies in healthcare through early genetic testing.

  • CFO Kevin Feeley reported Q4 revenues of $95.3 million, driven by a 101% year-over-year increase in exome and genome revenues. He noted sequential growth of 31% and highlighted a favorable reimbursement shift, with the average rate increasing to $3,500 per test. Feeley also pointed out that adjusted gross profit reached $66.9 million with a margin of 70%, up from 64% in the previous quarter.

Outlook

  • GeneDx provided 2025 revenue guidance of $350 million to $360 million, projecting at least 30% growth in exome and genome volume and revenue. Feeley indicated that the company expects to maintain profitability on an adjusted net income basis throughout 2025.

  • Feeley highlighted plans to achieve full-year adjusted gross margins between 65% and 67%, driven by ongoing cost optimizations and operational efficiencies.

Financial Results

  • Q4 adjusted net income reached $16.8 million, marking the second consecutive quarter of profitability. The company attributed this performance to strong reimbursement rates and operational improvements.

  • Exome and genome revenues contributed $78.8 million in Q4, supported by a 32% year-over-year increase in test volume. These tests accounted for 38% of all tests performed, up from 33% in the prior quarter.

  • GeneDx reported a total cash balance of $142.2 million as of December 31, 2024, supported by cash generated from operations and proceeds from its ATM program.

Q&A

  • Dan Brennan, TD Cowen: Asked about pricing assumptions for 2025 and expected profitability. CFO Feeley stated that rates are stable with expectations for further improvement, and OpEx will step up selectively to support growth while maintaining profitability.

  • William Bonello, Craig-Hallum Capital Group: Inquired about sales force expansion and cost implications. Stueland disclosed the sales team’s growth, including inside sales hires, with minimal incremental costs impacting OpEx.

  • Matt Stanton, Jefferies: Questioned the impact of the ultraRapid genome product on market penetration. Stueland explained the NICU market opportunity of approximately $1 billion, emphasizing the underutilization of genetic testing in this segment.

  • Mark Massaro, BTIG: Asked about the exit from hereditary cancer testing. Feeley clarified the majority of its contribution would be absent from 2025 revenues.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about growth opportunities, particularly in the NICU and outpatient settings. Questions reflected interest in pricing, profitability, and new market penetration.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, consistently emphasizing operational discipline and strategic growth. Stueland described the company as "running fast" to capitalize on its first-mover advantage.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone reflected heightened confidence in accelerating growth through new product launches and expanded market opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased sequentially from $76.6 million in Q3 to $95.3 million in Q4, with gross margins improving from 64% to 70%.

  • Management provided more detailed guidance for 2025, including specific targets for revenue growth and profitability, compared to broader projections in Q3.

  • Analysts’ questions shifted from general growth drivers to more detailed inquiries about sales force investments, NICU market strategies, and reimbursement improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing challenges with reimbursement rates, noting that nearly half of all tests are denied. Feeley emphasized efforts to reduce administrative denials and improve workflows.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the phasing of new product rollouts and their contribution to 2025 revenue growth, particularly in the NICU segment.

  • The company’s exit from hereditary cancer testing may create short-term revenue gaps, though management highlighted its strategic focus on exome and genome testing.

Final Takeaway

GeneDx Holdings Corp. delivered strong Q4 2024 results, achieving record revenues and profitability while setting ambitious targets for 2025. The company is doubling down on its market leadership in exome and genome testing, supported by innovative product launches like its ultraRapid genome sequencing. With a clear focus on operational efficiency and market expansion, GeneDx is poised for sustained growth in a largely untapped $25 billion pediatric testing market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Chemours projects $825M-$975M adjusted EBITDA for 2025 with focus on cost savings
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Chemours Company (NYSE:CC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Denise Dignam highlighted double-digit growth in Opteon Refrigerants, driven by regulatory transitions such as the US AIM Act and EU F-gas regulations. The company completed a capacity expansion at its Corpus Christi site to support increased demand.
  • The TT Transformation Plan exceeded its savings target for 2024, delivering $140 million in annual savings, with plans to achieve another $60 million in run-rate savings by the end of 2025.
  • The APM segment showed improvement in its Teflon PFA resin production line, which targets semiconductor customers, while weaker demand in hydrogen markets and economically sensitive sectors impacted sales.
  • CFO Shane Hostetter reported Q4 consolidated net sales of $1.4 billion, driven by a 2% volume increase offset by a 3% pricing decline. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $179 million, reflecting cost savings and operational efficiencies.
  • Hostetter also noted the remediation of four material weaknesses in internal control, marking significant progress for the company.

Outlook

  • Chemours forecasts adjusted EBITDA for 2025 to range between $825 million and $975 million. The higher end assumes favorable macroeconomic conditions, while the lower end anticipates regulatory and cost pressures.
  • For Q1 2025, consolidated net sales are expected to be flat to slightly down sequentially, with adjusted EBITDA also slightly down. Corporate expenses are anticipated to decrease by 30% compared to Q4 2024.
  • TSS expects sequential net sales growth in Q1, led by higher Opteon Refrigerant volumes, but margins may be impacted by increased costs tied to ramping up the new Corpus Christi capacity.
  • TT anticipates stable volumes but lower Q1 net sales due to regional mix shifts and operational downtime.
  • APM foresees continued softness in demand across hydrogen and semiconductor markets, with adjusted EBITDA decreasing sequentially due to weaker product mix and higher costs from scheduled maintenance.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net sales reached $1.4 billion, a 1% decline year-over-year, driven by pricing challenges across all segments. Adjusted EBITDA increased to $179 million from $176 million in the prior year.
  • Adjusted net income for Q4 was $16 million or $0.11 per diluted share, compared to $0.31 per diluted share in Q4 2023, reflecting lower effective tax benefits.
  • TSS set a quarterly sales record of $390 million, led by 23% growth in Opteon Refrigerants. TT's adjusted EBITDA rose 20% year-over-year, driven by cost savings from the transformation plan.
  • APM adjusted EBITDA grew 20% in Q4, aided by favorable inventory adjustments, but sales declined due to pricing and demand pressures.

Q&A

  • Arun Viswanathan, RBC Capital Markets: Questioned the $40-$50 million implied growth in 2025 EBITDA guidance. CFO Shane Hostetter attributed the increase to Opteon adoption, cost reductions, and operational improvements.
  • Josh Spector, UBS: Asked about TT regional mix headwinds. CEO Dignam clarified the mix shift is temporary and expected to reverse with seasonal coatings market recovery in the US by Q2.
  • Hassan Ahmed, Alembic Global Advisors: Inquired about Chinese TiO2 supply impacts. Dignam noted declining exports to Europe and Brazil, creating opportunities for Chemours.
  • John McNulty, BMO: Sought clarity on Freon pricing stability. Hostetter indicated Freon pricing is expected to remain at current levels into 2025 as HFC inventories persist.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism about APM's performance and TT's regional mix, signaling a slightly negative tone overall. Questions focused on regulatory risks, Freon pricing dynamics, and TT market recovery.
  • Management remained optimistic about Opteon growth and cost savings but acknowledged challenges, particularly in APM. Dignam projected confidence with phrases like "we are confident" and "we are well-positioned," though Hostetter's responses suggested cautious optimism in navigating headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 guidance language shifted to emphasize cost reductions and operational efficiency rather than macroeconomic stabilization highlighted in Q3.
  • Management's tone was more defensive regarding APM, reflecting ongoing challenges in hydrogen and semiconductor markets.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused on TT’s volume and pricing trends, contrasting with Q3’s emphasis on TSS growth.
  • Adjusted EBITDA improved sequentially in Q4, showcasing effective cost controls despite lower sales.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistent pricing pressures in Freon products due to elevated HFC inventories.
  • Regulatory uncertainty impacting APM and broader market conditions.
  • Macroeconomic headwinds in economically sensitive markets, including hydrogen and semiconductors.
  • Operational challenges such as scheduled maintenance and forced outages affecting short-term margins.

Final Takeaway

Chemours demonstrated resilience in Q4 2024, with strong performance in TSS and continued cost savings under the TT Transformation Plan. The company’s 2025 guidance reflects confidence in Opteon adoption and operational efficiency but acknowledges potential headwinds in APM and broader market conditions. Strategic cost reductions and focus on high-growth opportunities position Chemours for improved long-term profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: United Rentals is viewed favorably after being priced out of the H&E acquisition
data.description:
United Rentals (NYSE:URI) is being sized up by investors after equipment rental supplier Herc Holdings (HRI) boxed out the company by announcing a binding agreement to acquire H&E Equipment Services (HEES).

Separately, United Rentals (NYSE:URI) said it does not intend to submit a revised proposal for the acquisition of H&E, which permits the latter to terminate a previously announced merger agreement with United Rentals (URI).

"One of our key responsibilities as a management team is to be good stewards of our investors’ capital and our decision not to increase our offer for H&E reflects our commitment to financial discipline," updated CEO Matthew Flannery.

Looking ahead, he said the equipment rental company remains focused on leveraging its one-stop shop strategy, supported by world-class service and innovative solutions. The URI strategy will be to focus on profitable growth, strong free cash flow generation, and prudent capital allocation to drive compelling long-term value for shareholders. United Rentals (URI) plans to immediately restart its share buyback program.

Evercore ISI analyst David Raso thinks United Rentals (URI) management made the right decision. While the H&E deal at $4.8 billion was seen as making sense, the expected rate of return was called unattractive at the new HERC offer price. The firm kept an Outperform rating on United Rentals (URI) in place and backed its price target of $1,004.

Shares of United Rentals (URI) were down 3.6% in late morning trading on Tuesday.
